reasoning: Critical correctness failure: Q1 produces 1 row instead of 4 due to improper dictionary encoding handling. Root cause identified and fixable. Two critical fixes are required: (1) Load and use dictionary metadata to decode l_returnflag and l_linestatus from uint8_t indices to character values, (2) Use precise date threshold calculation. Performance is competitive (90ms vs DuckDB 86ms), but incorrect results are unacceptable. Must fix correctness before pursuing performance optimizations.
focus_areas: Fix dictionary encoding for l_returnflag and l_linestatus columns
Load dictionary metadata from GenDB storage and decode enum values before grouping
Compute exact date threshold (1998-09-02 = 10472 days) instead of hardcoded 10500
Verify output produces all 4 expected result rows with correct aggregate values
strategy_notes: Priority 1: Apply both critical fixes (recommendations 0 and 1). The dictionary encoding bug is fundamental—the code must load dictionary files (lineitem/l_returnflag.dict, lineitem/l_linestatus.dict), create lookup maps (uint8_t → char), and decode values before comparison in insert_or_update(). This is a semantic correctness issue, not optimization. Once correctness is established and 4 rows are produced, iteration 2 can focus on performance optimizations (parallelism for 60M row scan, I/O prefetching, potential SIMD for aggregation). Do NOT proceed with performance work while correctness is broken.
notes: Execution time (90ms) is actually good relative to DuckDB (86ms minimum), suggesting the core algorithm is sound. The bottleneck is semantic—incorrect dictionary handling. After fixing, verify all 4 group combinations (A,F), (A,O), (N,O), (R,O) appear in output with correct aggregate sums before pursuing further optimization. Remaining budget (2 iterations): use iteration 1 for correctness, iteration 2 for performance if needed.
